HOW I POLISH SOFTWARE THAT AGENTS BUILT https://every.


POLISH IS THE FINAL STEP OF COMPOUND ENGINEERING: USING THE APP MYSELF AND TELLING THE AGENT WHAT FEELS OFF UNTIL IT DOESN'T https://every.

by Kieran Klaassen https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEkgUG9saXNoIFNvZnR3YXJlIFRoYXQgQWdlbnRzIEJ1aWx0IiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzOCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQGtpZXJhbl8xMzU1IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjR9

Midjourney/Every illustration.

Was this newsletter forwarded to you? Sign up https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEkgUG9saXNoIFNvZnR3YXJlIFRoYXQgQWdlbnRzIEJ1aWx0IiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzOCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vYWNjb3VudCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo1fQ== to get it in your inbox.



----------------------------------------



For most of the history of making software, writing code was the work. You spent your days typing out functions, fighting errors, debugging migrations, and reading through other peopleвЂ™s code to figure out why a function did what it did. Another day in the code factory.

ThatвЂ™s mostly over. With a good brainstorm and a well-structured plan https://every., todayвЂ™s models will write code, run tests, fix failures, and hand you back a clean pull request with code changes ready for review. IвЂ™ll open my laptop in the morning to a stack of green pull requests that agents shipped overnight, with features ready to merge before my first meeting. The factory has been automated.

What IвЂ™m left to do is sit with the result and decide whether itвЂ™s any goodвЂ”and then push it further than the agent could take it itself. A model doesnвЂ™t know what I would call good, sitting in front of this feature in this product IвЂ™m building for these users. Without that final human judgment, everything the agent produces is functional, but forgettable. A few weeks ago I clicked on an email card in Cora https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEkgUG9saXNoIFNvZnR3YXJlIFRoYXQgQWdlbnRzIEJ1aWx0IiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzOCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vY29yYS5jb21wdXRlciIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo3fQ== and knew instantly the animation was wrongвЂ”it slid in from the top of the screen instead of opening from where IвЂ™d clicked mid-screen. I told the agent, it fixed it, and I clicked againвЂ”better.

Polish is the final step in compound engineering https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEkgUG9saXNoIFNvZnR3YXJlIFRoYXQgQWdlbnRzIEJ1aWx0IiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzOCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vZ3VpZGVzL2NvbXBvdW5kLWVuZ2luZWVyaW5nIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjh9вЂ”the discipline IвЂ™ve been developing over the past year for building software in a way that gets smarter with every iteration. As more of the middle parts of the process https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEkgUG9saXNoIFNvZnR3YXJlIFRoYXQgQWdlbnRzIEJ1aWx0IiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzOCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vd3d3LnlvdXR1YmUuY29tL3dhdGNoP3Y9RzBMVHY4aFE1Q3MiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OX0= gets automated, polish matters more now, because itвЂ™s the part of the work thatвЂ™s still yours. Instead of the assembly line worker putting the code together, youвЂ™re the person at the end of the line who decides whether itвЂ™s good enough to put your name on.


POLISH IN PRACTICE

The card animation that looked wrong is a good example of how polish works, because animation is something that agents still struggle with (although it will improve over time).

The pull request had passed every automated check. The review agents had nothing left to flag for me. I ran /ce-polishвЂ”part of the compound engineering plugin https://every.вЂ”which does three things and then gets out of my way. It checks out the branch I want to polish, starts the development server in the background, and opens the running app inside my editor, right next to the agent. From there, I clicked on an email card to open it and started my assessment.

https://every. updated version of the Cora Brief in development, which I refined using the polish step. (Image courtesy of Kieran Klaassen.)




Once the new design was open beside the agent, I could see that it still felt too loose. There was too much whitespace, and the cards needed to be more compact. I said so out loud via Monologue https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEkgUG9saXNoIFNvZnR3YXJlIFRoYXQgQWdlbnRzIEJ1aWx0IiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzOCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vbW9ub2xvZ3VlLnRvIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEyfQ==. The agent tightened the layout; the app hot-reloaded; I looked againвЂ”better. A plan could tell the agent to make the Brief feel like a magazine. It couldnвЂ™t tell the agent when the page had the right density. I had to see it and decide for myself.

This is what polish looks like: a conversation between me, the running app, and the agent. The whole setupвЂ”/c

MINI-VIBE CHECK: GROK 4.5 IS FAST, CHEAP, AND FINALLY USEFUL

Almost a year ago, we vibe-checked Grok 4 https://every. and found a model that performed well on benchmarks but was not useful enough for our engineers to use every day. Our April Vibe Check of Cursor 3.0 https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vdmliZS1jaGVjay9jdXJzb3IiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6N30= found a promising but unfinished agent-orchestration product, but that verdict aged quickly: Kieran Klaassen https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQGtpZXJhbl8xMzU1IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjh9 was using Cursor daily by the end of the month, and by June Composer 2.5 was his main model for final polish. Then, in June, SpaceX signed an agreement to acquire Cursor https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vd3d3LmNuYmMuY29tLzIwMjYvMDYvMTYvc3BhY2V4LXNwY3gtY3Vyc29yLWFjcXVpc2l0aW9uLWlwby5odG1sIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjl9, and a new candidate for the AI frontier space race was born.

Grok 4.5 https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8veC5haS9uZXdzL2dyb2stNC01IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEwfQ== is the first result of that collaboration. https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8veC5haS9uZXdzL2dyb2stNC01IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjExfQ== Cursor says it jointly trained the model https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vY3Vyc29yLmNvbS9ibG9nL2dyb2stNC01IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEyfQ== with SpaceXAI using data from interactions with codebases and software tools.

When our team ran Grok 4.5 through the evals we use internally, the consensus was that it is an Opus-level model. Mike Taylor https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQG1pa2VfMjExNCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxM30=вЂ™s latest benchmark put it slightly above Claude Opus 4.8 https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vdmliZS1jaGVjay9vcHVzLTQtOC12aWJlY2hlY2siLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTR9: Grok followed every step and returned a complete, polished result, while Opus stopped early or skipped parts of the assignment.

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8veC5jb20vaGFtbWVyX210L3N0YXR1cy8yMDc0OTQxODY5MDYzMDMzMTk2IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjE1fQ==Mike shared his early impressions of Grok-4.5 on X, including what he deems Opus-level PowerPoint creation. (Image courtesy of X/Mike Taylor.)




Kieran ran Grok 4.5 through /LFG, EveryвЂ™s compound-engineering https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vZ3VpZGVzL2NvbXBvdW5kLWVuZ2luZWVyaW5nIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjE2fQ== workflow for planning, building, reviewing, and improving a project. He put it around ClaudeвЂ™s Opus 4.5 https://every.-to-4.6 https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRnJvbSBEb2luZyB0byBUZW5kaW5nIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMzNywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vdmliZS1jaGVjay9vcHVzLTQtNiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxOH0= range, calling it вЂњnot state of the art, but pretty good for a lot of things, and very fast.вЂќ

GrokвЂ™s biggest competitive advantage may be its impact on the bottom line: xAI says Grok 4.5 runs at roughly 80 tokens per second and about twice the token efficiency of leading models. https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvLyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjowfQ==

Chain of Thought

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NoYWluLW9mLXRob3VnaHQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MX0=


HOW GPT-5.6 CHANGES KNOWLEDGE WORK https://every.


DONвЂ™T DO YOUR WORK. TEND YOUR LOOP. https://every.

by Dan Shipper https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL0BkYW5zaGlwcGVyIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjR9

Midjourney/Every illustration.

TL;DR: GPT-5.6 is the first model IвЂ™ve used that can reliably run whole loops of knowledge work, not just help with individual tasks. Your job turns from doing the work to tending the system that does it. If you want to try this for yourself, weвЂ™re open-sourcing a prompt and repository called Tend https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL3RlbmQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NX0= so you can try working this way in ChatGPT Work.

Experiment with Tend https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL3RlbmQ_c291cmNlPXBvc3RfYnV0dG9uIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjZ9



----------------------------------------



GPT-5.6 heralds a new way of doing knowledge work.

Instead of using AI to complete one task at a time, you build a system that scans the available information, turns it into proposed decisions, and carries out the ones you approve. Over time, the system compounds your feedback to do more and more on its own.

This changes your job. It requires you to see your work asвЂ”dare I say itвЂ”a loop https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2NvbnRleHQtd2luZG93L2xvb3BzLWZvci1ub24tY29kZXJzIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjd9. You go from doing all of the work yourself to tending the system that does it for you:

https://every. knowledge work loop, with human judgment at the center. (Image courtesy of Dan Shipper/Claude/GPT Image.)




Take email. I used to go through my inbox like this: Email comes in, I read it, I reply, I archive. (Or, email comes in, I open it, I close it, I wait several weeks, I open it again, I archive it.)

With GPT-5.6 Sol https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL3ZpYmUtY2hlY2svZ3B0LTUtNiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo5fQ== in the new ChatGPT Work app, formerly known as Codex, the process looks very different. GPT-5.6 Sol watches my inbox, decides what deserves my attention, does any necessary research, and presents each email with a concise summary and proposed reply. I either approve the draft or use Monologue https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL3d3dy5tb25vbG9ndWUudG8vIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEwfQ== to dictate what I want changed. Then I move to the next email.

At the end of each sweep through my inbox, the agent derives my preferences from my revisions and decisions and remembers them for next time.

ItвЂ™s no coincidence this sounds like compound engineering https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiSG93IEdQVC01LjYgQ2hhbmdlcyBLbm93bGVkZ2UgV29yayIsInBvc3RfaWQiOjQzMzYsInBvc3RfdHlwZSI6InBvc3QiLCJ1cmwiOiJodHRwczovL2V2ZXJ5LnRvL2d1aWRlcy9jb21wb3VuZC1lbmdpbmVlcmluZyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMX0=; itвЂ™s the same philosophyвЂ”just applied to knowledge work. IвЂ™ve been writing about this shift for a few years: In early 2024 I argued that much knowledge work would become managing agents https://every., and later that it would look like tending to a garden https://every.вЂ”creating the conditions for work to happen, rather than doing everything yourself directly. This isnвЂ™t a new way of working: Managers and entrepreneurs have done it for decades, and as models improved over the past year, programmers adopted it too. Now itвЂ™s knowledge workвЂ™s turn.

This approach wonвЂ™t work for every kind of knowledge work, and itвЂ™s still early. But where it works, it creates a remarkable kind of leverage.


WHAT MAKES GPT-5.6 SOL AND CHATGPT WORK DIFFERENT

GPT-5.6 Sol crosses the threshold that makes a continuous knowledge-work loop practical. It can scan your sources, identify whatвЂ™s relevant, carry out approved work, and build custom tools for itself as needed. It can do all of this reliably even if you canвЂ™t codeвЂ”and it can explain all of this to you in a way thatвЂ™s understandable.

ItвЂ™s also fast and cheap enough that you can iterate rapidlyвЂ”a crucial requirement for non-technical users who are going to make mistakes and need to see the results of a run before knowing i

----------

happy friday, AI family, and welcome back to AI Valley.

**here are the biggest things worth knowing today:**

* OpenAIвЂ™s new вЂњSuper AppвЂќ wants to be your co-worker

* OpenAI just made ChatGPT conversations feel more human

* 1X unveiled one of the most advanced humanoid robot hands

* Muse Spark 1.1 debuts as MetaвЂ™s advanced coding model

* Plus trending AI tools, posts, and resources

LetвЂ™s _dive_ into the Valley of AIвЂ¦


----------

----------
#### **VOICES**

Building production-grade AI voice starts long before model trainingвЂ”it starts with sourcing, licensing, and structuring your data. That part shouldn't take months.

The Voices Dataset Catalogue gives AI and ML teams immediate access to professionally directed Voice Data: character performances spanning 450+ unique types, expressive speech across 43 emotional states, and multilingual recordings across 8 languages (part of 100,000+ hours of premium Voice Data available from Voices).

Every dataset is fully consented, production-ready, and structured for immediate pipeline integration.

Three specialized datasets. Real range. Zero sourcing headaches.

[Request a Sample в†’](https://www.voices.com/landing/off-the-shelf-catalogue?utm_source=ai_valley&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=client_ots-bulk-sales-play_2026)

~_*This is sponsored_~


----------

----------
#### **THROUGH THE VALLEY**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/819080f2-3abe-4b50-9e42-55b62ec48eb0/image_-_2026-07-10T200328.714__1_.png?t=1783694422)
Caption: Courtesy: Anthropic

**1/ OpenAIвЂ™s new вЂњSuper AppвЂќ wants to be your co-worker: **The company launched [ChatGPT Work](https://openai.com/index/chatgpt-for-your-most-ambitious-work/), a GPT-5.6-powered workspace that combines chat, coding, automation, and workplace integrations to complete complex tasks with minimal supervision. Connected to tools like Slack, Microsoft Teams, Google Drive, SharePoint, and Salesforce, it can work for hours on multi-step projects, bringing OpenAI into direct competition with Anthropic's Claude Cowork and Microsoft Copilot. ([watch the announcement live stream here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Wq45rvPGNHs)_)_.

**2/ OpenAI just made ChatGPT conversations feel more human:** The company launched [GPT-Live](https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-live), a new full-duplex voice model that allows ChatGPT to listen and speak simultaneously, enabling natural interruptions, faster responses, and more fluid back-and-forth conversations. The new experience also supports real-time interruptions, memory, search, and GPT-5.5-powered responses, bringing voice interactions closer to speaking with another person. ([watch the full livestream here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9f-Ew_lDtxc))

**3/ GPT-5.6 rolls out broadly as OpenAIвЂ™s new top AI model:** The company [launched](https://openai.com/index/gpt-5-6) GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna, a new family of models built for coding, reasoning, cybersecurity, and enterprise workloads. OpenAI says its flagship Sol outperforms Anthropic's Fable 5 while running faster, using 54% fewer tokens on agentic coding tasks, and costing roughly one-third less. GPT-5.6 Sol has been [ranked 2nd](https://x.com/ArtificialAnlys/status/2075268970492657905) to AnthropicвЂ™s Claude Fable 5 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index. ([watch the launch here](https://x.com/OpenAI/status/2075271421149020426?s=20))

**4/ Anthropic is turning AI usage into something you can measure: **The company launched a new [reflection dashboard](https://x.com/claudeai/status/2075225957711929667?s=20) for Claude that analyzes conversations across recurring topics, task types, and usage patterns over the past year. Alongside privacy-focused insights, the feature adds quiet hours, break reminders, and self-reflection prompts to help users build healthier AI habits.

**5/ 1X just unveiled one of the most advanced humanoid robot hands yet: **The company's new [NEO hand](https://x.com/1x_tech/status/2075252899442204952?s=20) features 25 degrees of freedom, force sensing in every joint, tactile skin, and a lightweight tendon-driven design that closely mimics human dexterity. Built to be waterproof, durable, and mass-manufacturable, 1X says it already has the capacity to produce up to 10,000 hands this year.

**6/ SpaceXAI and Cursor just launched Grok's biggest upgrade yet:** The companies unveiled [Grok 4.5](https://x.ai/news/grok-4-5), a new flagship model that delivers Opus-class performance across coding, agentic, and knowledge tasks while running significantly faster and at a fraction of the cost. With benchmark results approaching Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5, Grok 4.5 positions itself as one of the strongest value-focused frontier AI models available.

**7/ Muse Spark 1.1 debuts as MetaвЂ™s advanced coding model:** The company launched [Muse Spark 1.1](https://x.com/alexandr_wang/status/2075218936266998230?s=20) alongside the public preview of the Meta Model API, bringing a multimodal reasoning model with a 1 million-token context window, advanced agent workflows, and stronger coding capabilities. Meta says the model delivers major improvements over its predecessor while enabling developers to build AI agents that can use tools, browse apps, and complete complex tasks autonomously.

VIBE CHECK: GPT-5.6 SOL IS OUR FAVORITE MODEL TO COLLABORATE WITH https://every.


SOL IS FAST, RESOURCEFUL, AND UNUSUALLY EASY TO STEERвЂ”BUT FABLE STILL GETS THE ASSIGNMENTS WE WANT TO HAND OFF COMPLETELY https://every.

by Katie Parrott https://every.

Midjourney/Every illustration.

This is a free preview of a subscribers-only post.

Was this newsletter forwarded to you? Sign up to get it in your inbox.



----------------------------------------



At the end of June, we lost access to GPT-5.6 Sol while it went through government review. Dan Shipper said returning to older models felt like going back to the Stone Age. Austin Tedesco compared GPT-5.5 to shooting a basketball twice as heavy.

Now Sol is back. ItвЂ™s become our default for work we want to shape as we go: writing, coding, research, and analysis inside OpenAIвЂ™s new unified ChatGPT and Codex desktop app. Sol is fast, unusually good at finding and using context, and easy to redirect. AnthropicвЂ™s Fable still leads on broad assignments that require the model to decide what should be built in the first place.

We get into all of it in our Vibe Check, including:

 * Why Sol scored 56/100 against FableвЂ™s 90/100 on our Senior Engineer benchmarkвЂ”and how 12,900 lines of unnecessary code explain most of the gap
 * How Kieran Klaassen rebuilt Proof with Sol in about one-third the time Fable needed, only to prefer FableвЂ™s design
 * Why Sol finished last in our six-model writing benchmark while I (Katie Parrott) still used it to move through 24 drafts of this article in six to eight hours
 * How Sol found Arielle ShipperвЂ™s email, inspected 46 CSV files, and returned with seven useful questionsвЂ”then made a calculation error serious enough to shake her trust
